content.problemStatement:
There are NNN friends numbered 111 to NNN, seated in a straight line where the ithi^{th}ith seat is taken by friend numbered AiA_iAi​.

This Christmas, the friends have decided to distribute candies amongst each other.

For each 1≤i<N1\le i \lt N1≤i<N, the friend at the ithi^{th}ith seat will gift **one** candy to the friend at the jthj^{th}jth seat if:


j>ij \gt ij>i ;
Aj<AiA_j \lt A_iAj​<Ai​
Find the number of people who receive **at least one** candy.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the number of friends.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of people who receive **at least one** candy.
constraints: 1 ≤ Ai ≤ N, all Ai are distinct
Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the number of friends. The next line contains N distinct integers A1 … AN – the permutation of 1..N describing the friend sitting at each seat.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the number of friends who receive at least one candy.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: array [1,2] – both positions are prefix maxima, so no one receives candy.
Test case 2: array [3,1,2] – only the first position is a prefix maximum; positions 2 and 3 each have a larger element before them, so they receive candy (2 friends).
Test case 3: array [2,3,4,1] – first three positions are increasing prefix maxima; only position 4 has a larger earlier element, so exactly 1 friend receives candy.
Only the first friend (value 5) is a prefix maximum. All other four friends have a larger value before them, so each receives at least one candy.
Prefix maxima occur at positions 1 (1), 2 (3), 4 (5), and 6 (6) – total 4. Hence 6‑4 = 2 friends (positions 3 and 5) receive candies.

solution.approach:
A friend at position j receives a candy if there exists an earlier position i (i < j) with Ai > Aj. Therefore j does NOT receive a candy only when Aj is greater than every element before it – i.e., Aj is a new prefix maximum. While scanning the array from left to right we keep the current maximum value and count how many times this maximum is updated (including the first element). Let cnt be the number of prefix‑maximum positions. The answer is N - cnt.

Algorithm per test case:
1. Read N.
2. Initialise maxVal = -1, prefixMaxCnt = 0.
3. For each element x in the array (index i from 0):
   - if i == 0 or x > maxVal: set maxVal = x and increment prefixMaxCnt.
4. Output N - prefixMaxCnt.

The scan is O(N) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
